data.title: THEMAC receives court approval for acquisition by Tulla Resources
data.description:
• THEMAC Resources Group (TSXV:MAC:CA) has received the final order from the Yukon Supreme Court approving its acquisition by Tulla Resources Group Pty.
  • Under the plan of arrangement, Tulla will acquire all remaining common shares of THEMAC not already owned.
  • Shareholders will receive CAD $0.08 per share in cash.
  • The transaction is expected to close later this month.
  • Upon completion, THEMAC will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Tulla and its shares will be delisted from the TSX Venture Exchang.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: C1 Fund acquires stake in Ripple to boost blockchain investment portfolio
data.description:
• C1 Fund (NYSE:CFND) has announced a new investment in Ripple, a global enterprise blockchain company.
  • The Fund focuses on late-stage digital assets and blockchain infrastructure.
  • Ripple’s technology supports cross-border payments and modernizes financial infrastructure.
  • Ripple's platform uses RippleUSD stablecoin and the XRP Ledger for asset settlement.
  • XRPL is an open-source blockchain known for fast and scalable crypto-native and fiat transactions.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Western Uranium & Vanadium upsizes offering to C$5.9M
data.description:
• Western Uranium & Vanadium (OTCQX:WSTRF) has increased its previously announced offering from C$5M to C$5.9M.
  • A total of 6.56M units will be issued at C$0.90 per unit.
  • Each unit includes one common share and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Warrants are exercisable at C$1.20 per share for a period of 4.5 years from issuance.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Uniti subsidiaries price $250M fiber securitization notes offering
data.description:
Uniti Group (NASDAQ:UNIT) announced that its subsidiaries, Uniti Fiber ABS Issuer LLC and Uniti Fiber TRS Issuer LLC, have priced a $250 million offering of secured fiber network revenue term notes.

The issuance includes $180 million of 5.177% Class A-2 notes, $28.2 million of 5.621% Class B notes, and $41.8 million of 7.834% Class C notes, all due in January 2031. The combined offering carries a weighted average coupon rate of about 5.671%.

The notes are secured by Uniti’s fiber network assets and related customer contracts across Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi, and South Carolina. The offering is expected to close on October 24, 2025.

Following the close, Uniti’s subsidiaries plan to enter into a $75 million variable funding note facility with a delayed draw feature, subject to certain leverage and funding conditions.

Proceeds from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes, including capital expenditures and debt repayment.

data.title: PC market sees strong Q3 growth amid Windows 10 transition, says Omdia
data.description:
Global shipments of desktops, notebooks, and workstations grew 6.8% year-over-year to 72M units in Q3 2025, according to the latest Omdia research. The uptick is driven by a wave of upgrade activity ahead of Microsoft’s Windows 10 end-of-support deadline on October 14.

Notebook shipments, including mobile workstations, rose 4% to 57.2 million units, while desktop shipments (including desktop workstations) surged 17% to 15.2 million units.

Top 5 PC Vendors – Q3 2025 

  • Lenovo (OTCPK:LNVGY) retained its lead in the global PC market, shipping approximately 19.4 million units, marking a 17% year-over-year increase.
  • HP (NYSE:HPQ) secured second place with 15 million units shipped, achieving 11% growth from the previous year.
  • Dell (NYSE:DELL) ranked third, showing modest annual growth of around 3%, supported by ongoing refresh demand.
  • Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) held fourth place, recording 4.3% growth and surpassing the 6-million unit mark for the fifth consecutive quarter.
  • Asus (OTCPK:ASUUY) rounded out the top five with 5.8 million units shipped, up 7% year-on-year.

Omdia Research Director Ishan Dutt noted that “business and consumer demand for PC upgrades continues to be healthy,” with many users still on older systems or running Windows 10.

Despite strong marketing efforts, a recent Omdia poll of 453 channel partners revealed only 39% have upgraded nearly all customer PCs, while 18% plan to remain on Windows 10 post-deadline—likely opting for Microsoft's (MSFT) extended security updates.

As upgrade demand extends into Q4 and beyond, chipmakers and OEMs are gearing up with new products. Qualcomm (QCOM) launched the Snapdragon X2 Elite chipset, and Intel (INTC) announced its next-gen Panther Lake Core Ultra processors.

Omdia predicts that CES 2026 will showcase the next wave of AI-enabled PCs.

data.title: Northwest Natural raises dividend by 0.5% to $0.4925 a share
data.description:
• Northwest Natural Holding (NYSE:NWN) declares $0.4925/share quarterly dividend, 0.5% increase from prior dividend of $0.4900.
  • Forward yield 4.51%
  • Payable Nov. 14; to shareholders of record Oct. 31; ex-div Oct. 31.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 0.5% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.49 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See NWN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Apogee beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Apogee press release (NASDAQ:APOG): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.98 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $358.19M (+4.6% Y/Y) beats by $7.28M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA decreased to $44.4 million compared to $53.1 million, and adjusted EBITDA margin decreased to 12.4% compared to 15.5%.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities in the second quarter was $57.1 million, compared to $58.7 million in the prior-year period. 
  • Fiscal 2026 OutlookThe company now expects net sales in the range of $1.39 billion to $1.42 billion vs. consensus of $1.42B, and diluted EPS in the range of $2.79 to $3.19 and adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $3.60 to $3.90 vs. consensus of $3.96. This includes a projected unfavorable EPS impact from tariffs of $0.35 to $0.45. The company’s revised outlook assumes an effective tax rate of approximately 27%.
  • The company continues to assume capital expenditures between $35 million to $40 million.

data.title: Lockheed Martin raises dividend by 4.5% to $3.45 a share; approves $2B increase to share repurchase program
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) declares $3.45/share quarterly dividend, 4.5% increase from prior dividend of $3.30.
  • Forward yield 2.72%
  • Payable Dec. 30; to shareholders of record Dec. 1; ex-div Dec. 1.
  • The company's board officially approved adding $2 billion to its existing share repurchase program. Following this increase, Lockheed Martin now has roughly $9.1 billion authorized for future common stock repurchases.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 4.5% after paying a quarterly dividend of $3.30 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See LMT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: WD-40 declares $0.94 dividend
data.description:
• WD-40 (NASDAQ:WDFC) declares $0.94/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

data.title: Senate passes bill requiring Nvidia, AMD to prioritize domestic customers over China
data.description:
The U.S. Senate on Thursday passed bipartisan legislation requiring advanced AI chipmakers such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) to prioritize American customers over Chinese buyers, a move that could complicate the industry’s push to expand global sales.

The Guaranteeing Access and Innovation for National Artificial Intelligence (GAIN AI) Act is a proposed bill that aims to give domestic companies priority access to advanced AI chips by requiring domestic orders to be fulfilled first before any exports.

The measure, approved as part of the annual defense policy bill, aims to strengthen U.S. competitiveness in emerging technologies and limit exports to China and other adversaries, according to co-sponsor Senator Jim Banks, an Indiana Republican.

“Today, the Senate acted to make sure American customers — including small businesses and startups — aren’t forced to wait in line behind China’s tech giants when purchasing the latest AI chips,” said Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.), the bill’s Democratic co-sponsor.

Tech companies and trade groups have opposed the legislation, warning that it could stifle innovation and undercut U.S. chipmakers’ global competitiveness.

The bill’s future remains uncertain, as the House of Representatives passed its own version of the defense bill in September without the export-control provisions. Lawmakers from both chambers must now reconcile the differences before sending a final bill to President Donald Trump.

The Senate action comes months after the Trump administration reached a deal with Nvidia and AMD to ease chip export restrictions to China. Nvidia has argued the legislation targets a “nonexistent problem,” noting that the U.S. remains its largest market.

Still, advocates say the move is an important safeguard. The measure is a “major win for U.S. economic competitiveness and national security,” said Brad Carson, president of the nonprofit Americans for Responsible Innovation.
